skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Review pull requests using Sentry engineering practices for security, performance, testing, design, and maintainability feedback.
skill.summary: Guide structured code reviews using Sentry engineering practices.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance terms, and a hardcoded URL. Manual review found these are Markdown examples, review checklist prose, or a reference link, with no executable code or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown fenced code blocks and example delimiters. They do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside Markdown code fences and contain instructional examples. No script file, command runner, or user-controlled execution path exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Terms
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography matches appear to come from ordinary words in the description and heading text, not cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe code review scope and a design assessment heading. They do not reference cryptographic functions, algorithms, or implementation choices.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keywords
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reconnaissance matches are checklist and sample SQL text used to teach reviewers what to inspect. They do not collect system or network data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is review guidance and a SQL injection example. No evidence shows host enumeration, port scanning, or data discovery behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Link
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URL is a reference to Sentry code review guidance. It is not used for network requests or data transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in a References section. No executable code calls the URL or sends local data to it.

content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss risks when feedback is unstructured. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Sentry-style review checks.
actual_capabilities: Checks pull requests for runtime errors, performance risks, and side effects.
Reviews Django ORM changes for query behavior and N+1 risks.
Reviews React hooks for dependency and state management issues.
Flags security-sensitive changes for injection and access control concerns.
Guides feedback toward actionable, polite, and risk-focused comments.
limitations: Does not run tests or inspect repositories by itself.
Does not replace senior review for schema, API, or security-sensitive changes.
Focuses on Sentry engineering practices, which may not fit every team.
Requires the user to provide relevant diffs, files, or pull request context.

use_cases.title: Review a Backend Pull Request
use_cases.description: Find ORM, migration, API, and security issues before approving a backend change.

use_cases.title: Improve Frontend Review Quality
use_cases.description: Check React changes for hook dependencies, side effects, and missing tests.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Feedback
use_cases.description: Use a consistent checklist for actionable code review comments across a team.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pull Request Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request using the code-review skill. Focus on correctness, tests, and actionable feedback.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change

prompt_templates.title: Django Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Django diff for ORM query behavior, N+1 risks, migrations, and missing tests.

prompt_templates.title: React Side Effect Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React change for hook dependencies, stale state, unnecessary renders, and test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Sensitive Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change for injection, access control, secrets exposure, compatibility risks, and senior review triggers.

output_examples.input: A Django pull request adds a loop that reads each user's profile inside the loop.
output_examples.output: Finding: This may create an N+1 query pattern. Consider prefetching related profiles and adding a regression test.

output_examples.input: A React component fetches data in an effect but omits the user identifier dependency.
output_examples.output: Finding: The effect can use stale data when the user changes. Include the dependency or explain why it is stable.

output_examples.input: A pull request changes an API response shape without migration notes.
output_examples.output: Finding: This may break callers. Add compatibility notes, migration coverage, or a phased rollout plan.

best_practices: Provide the relevant diff, tests, and product context before asking for review.
Ask for severity-ranked findings when reviewing large or risky changes.
Use the output as review support, then verify issues against the actual codebase.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for style-only feedback when the goal is risk reduction.
Do not treat generated review comments as approval authority.
Do not omit tests or runtime context for behavior-changing pull requests.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews pull requests for correctness, performance, tests, security, compatibility, and design concerns.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is guidance text and does not include executable scripts or commands.

faq.question: Is it only for Sentry projects?
faq.answer: No. It follows Sentry practices, but many checks apply to Django, React, and general code review.

faq.question: Can it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure review, but humans must validate findings and make approval decisions.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: Provide the pull request diff, affected files, tests, and any expected behavior changes.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
